Peter, Our district is so gerrymandered it inspired a board game... You heard that right. Texas 10th constituent Josh Lafair and his siblings, created Mapmaker after learning about gerrymandering. “Scheming, strategizing, backstabbing — gerrymandering has all the right mechanics of a board game,” Josh told me. “Kind of unfortunately.”
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/6724851/61976542/203122784?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82MzkwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlODgyOWExOS04Mjc1LWU5MTEtYWJjNC0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=cTQJ0jtZ82Ze1RCmZiW_9OxX9vkHNfo6k76A3eUChcw=&refcode=em050719) **You know what I don’t think should be a game? Playing with people’s lives.** Gerrymandering is one of the biggest stains on our democracy. It does nothing more than allow our representatives to choose their voters and not the other way around. We are in a crisis. From the disastrous ruling of Citizens United to the wave of gerrymandering in 2011, our democracy is under attack. We need to get money out of politics and create free and fair elections. [**That is why I am rejected corporate PAC money. And, that is why I need your support to fight for free and fair elections in Congress.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/6724852/61976543/203122784?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82MzkwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlODgyOWExOS04Mjc1LWU5MTEtYWJjNC0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=cTQJ0jtZ82Ze1RCmZiW_9OxX9vkHNfo6k76A3eUChcw=&refcode=em050719) Our system is broken at a fundamental level. In Florida, voters gave the formerly incarcerated the right to vote and legislators are taking it back. In Georgia, people who are running for high office are also running those elections. All around the country, voter ID laws, the closing of polling stations in minority areas, and rampant gerrymandering are showing the cracks in our system. And, right here in Texas 10, our gerrymandered district inspired a board game. **I spent my life fighting for those without a voice as a public school teacher and civil rights attorney. Now, I am running for Congress to fight for our democracy.[Will you help me fight with a donation today?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/6724853/61976544/203122784?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82MzkwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlODgyOWExOS04Mjc1LWU5MTEtYWJjNC0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=cTQJ0jtZ82Ze1RCmZiW_9OxX9vkHNfo6k76A3eUChcw=&refcode=em050719)

Join me in fighting for our democracy. Join me in fighting for free and fair elections.**

In solidarity,
Mike Siegel

name: Gov. Mike Huckabee
subject: Don’t wait! Sail with me!
body:
| | Peter, There are times I’d love to sit around a table with you, have coffee, and chat about the things that are happening in our world, so I want to let you know about a unique opportunity where we will do just that. [August 9-18, we’ve chartered an entire ship to experience one of the most beautiful places on earth in Northern Europe, along some of the most picturesque cities along the Baltic Sea](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=FECXgvVBAH18GBWRCy8D9S_H0X-LMKxuVQZOVdM48FB6clUdS6lpIFIKWvxyXp9e2cOQvzJtyDDTF_AeDLkTIA&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190513com01&n=1). When it is so hot in most of the U.S. in August, we’ll enjoy pleasant weather and see places like Stockholm, Helsinki, Copenhagen, the surprising country of Estonia (one of my personal favorites that really surprised me), and we’ll even do some REAL Russian collusion when we visit the stunning city of St. Petersburg. It’s more than sightseeing, although that would be worth the trip. We will be able to contrast freedom from socialism and talk to some of the people who lived under it in places like St. Petersburg and Estonia. Senator Rick Santorum and economist Dan Celia will join me to lead discussions while at sea and to visit with you along the way. Rick Santorum is a good friend as is Dan Celia who is one of the few financial guys who can actually make sense of the economy. We will also be bringing incredible musicians, singers, and entertainers to make it fun. [If you’ve been on any of my Israel trips or our Mediterranean cruise last year, you know we include EVERYTHING in the price](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=FECXgvVBAH18GBWRCy8D9S_H0X-LMKxuVQZOVdM48FB6clUdS6lpIFIKWvxyXp9e2cOQvzJtyDDTF_AeDLkTIA&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190513com01&n=2)! ALL meals, onboard amenities, excursions, and more. There will be some options if you want to do other shore events, but you won’t have to pull out your credit card for other things once you get on the plane to meet us in Stockholm where we will begin. The ship is a luxury 5-Star vessel known for over-the-top pampering. We have the entire ship, so you’ll be with like-minded people on a ship that has every imaginable perk but is not so large as to lose the intimacy and sense of community. It will also give me an opportunity to personally interact with you while on the cruise. subject: President Trump is Right on China, and Now is the Time for Congress to Act
body:
"For far too long China has repeatedly threatened U.S. economic and national security interests." | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=f152ca2f99&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **President Trump is Right on China, and Now is the Time for Congress to Act**

“President Trump understands that now is the time to hold China to account. For far too long China has repeatedly threatened U.S. economic and national security interests. Finally, we have a president who is serious about getting tough on China and fighting for reciprocal trade deals,” Bradley Blakeman writes in Fox News.

“In order to keep the pressure on China, now is the time for Democrats and Republicans in Congress to pass the trade agreement between the U.S., Canada and Mexico, (USMCA). There is no doubt that if House Speaker Nancy Pelosi, D-Calif., gave it her green light it would pass the House.”

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=b4c8b94ff2&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| In [Fox News](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=2cf8de40a9&e=3ee4985eea), Sen. Mike Braun (R-IN) writes that Democrats’ absurd claims about Attorney General William Barr reveal a sad truth about President Obama’s Attorneys General Eric Holder and Loretta Lynch—and about Washington in general. “Democrats’ public vilification of Barr is a glimpse into how Holder’s ‘Fast and Furious’ scandal or Loretta Lynch’s tarmac summit with Bill Clinton might have played out without the air support of a breathless liberal media unwilling to portray Obama officials as anything but selfless public servants and Trump officials as anything but supervillains.”
---
|
---
| Next month, the MISSION Act signed by President Trump “will enable VA to consolidate the department’s community care efforts into a single, simple-to-use program that will empower veterans with the ability to choose the health care providers they trust,” [Secretary of Veterans Affairs Robert Wilkie](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=db53b2c00c&e=3ee4985eea) writes in Fox News. So what can veterans expect? “Less red tape, more satisfaction and predictability for patients, more efficiency for our clinicians, and better value for taxpayers.”
---
|
---
| At the White House last week, “President Donald Trump announced his principles to solve a huge problem facing our healthcare system: surprise medical bills. Present at the event were victims of outrageously large, surprise bills and their family members,” [Joe Grogan, Assistant to the President for Domestic Policy](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=6315c4184a&e=3ee4985eea), writes in CNN. “Unlike Senator Bernie Sanders' Medicare for All legislation that would take away Americans' ability to choose their insurance and eliminate all private coverage, President Trump wants people to be able to keep insurance plans they like while no longer facing the issue of surprise medical bills.”
---
|

[Privacy Policy](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=5b4157415d&e=3ee4985eea) | [Contact the White House](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=d97230d964&e=3ee4985eea) | [Unsubscribe](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=fc6bc34d8a)

Dear Friend –
As we learn more about Georgia’s choice ban, Teresa is out in front and leading the fight for a woman’s right to choose. This weekend, she joined MSNBC to talk about the unconstitutional nature of the law, and how Republican legislators are restricting the constitutional right to choose in order to force the courts’ hands in overturning it.
Watch the clip here: [https://teresatomlinson.com/watch-teresa-on-msnbcs-morning-joy/?refcode=051319]
https://teresatomlinson.com/watch-teresa-on-msnbcs-morning-joy/?refcode=051319 [https://teresatomlinson.com/watch-teresa-on-msnbcs-morning-joy/?refcode=051319]
Teresa explains: “ It’s an extreme effort to overturn Roe v. Wade – and patently unconstitutional...These legislators need to stop practicing medicine without a license. We knew that elected Republican officials struggled with science, as they handled climate change. We knew that they struggle with math, as they dealt with our deficit. And now we know that they know nothing about biology. ”
If this case goes all the way to the top, we’re about to see whether we have jurists or ideologues on the Supreme Court.
Watch Teresa on MSNBC here. [https://teresatomlinson.com/watch-teresa-on-msnbcs-morning-joy/?refcode=051319]

Onward!

name: Kamala Harris
subject: A crisis of confidence in our government
body:
Our federal government was built on a system of checks and balances -- and
since Trump was elected, we have seen what happens when our system is
pushed to its limits. I have seen it up close while serving in the Senate:
Republican members of Congress refuse to uphold their duty to serve as a
check on this administration.

Let’s speak this truth: many hard-working Americans have grown distrustful
of our government, our institutions, and our leaders -- and rightly so.
I’ve heard it time and again while speaking with voters in communities
across our country. This is not just a crisis of confidence in our
government -- we potentially face a constitutional crisis.

I’m running for president
because the American people deserve truth and integrity from our elected
leaders. And let me say: I fully intend to win this election.

From day one, I have pledged to run our campaign differently. A campaign
for the people must be funded by the people, not corporate PACs or federal
lobbyists. In short, we won’t win without you. That’s why I’m asking: can
you add a contribution to power our
campaign?

Winning this election will depend on millions of people stepping up and
getting into the ring, raising our voices together to fight for our
values. It’s on all of us, together, to fight for the future of our
country.

Together, we are fighting to pass the most significant middle-class tax
break we have seen in generations -- made possible by reversing the Trump
tax cuts for the wealthiest 1% and putting money back in the hands of the
middle class.

Together, we are fighting to make the largest investment in teachers in
American history and provide the average teacher a $13,500 raise --
entirely closing the teacher pay gap.

Together, we are fighting to enact Medicare for All and guarantee health
care as a right for every single American -- not a privilege for those who
can afford it.

Together, we are fighting to address climate change with real solutions to
protect our planet for generations to come.

Together, we are fighting to address gun violence to make our communities
safer; Lift millions of Americans out of poverty; And live up to the
promise of what our country can be -- unburdened by what has been.

If we want to make our vision a reality and give the American people a
reason to believe in our government again, we need to fight like hell to
win this election. I’m counting on you.

clean_body: | "" \---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- "" Peter, I'm sorry to be so abrupt. But Donald Trump has over $40 million in the bank. And unlike him, I'm relying on grassroots supporters like you to power my campaign. Our mid-month deadline is only 48 hours away, and we are still 8,000 donations away from our goal. If we are going to start to close the gap between our campaign and his, we can't fall short. Peter, if you were looking for a time to make your first donation, this is it. Can you chip in $5 today? | | "" | "" | "" | "" | "" | "" Right now we are seeing incredible abuses of power from this White House. I know it makes some of you feel like America's best days are behind us. But Peter, it 's never a good idea to bet against America. You may say I'm being too optimistic, but I believe I'm being thoroughly realistic. My confidence is born out of my own experience, and America's experience - that when given half a chance, the American people have never, ever let their country down. I am more optimistic today than I've ever been because of you, and because of who we are. But Peter, I need you by my side if we are going to restore the soul of the nation and get this country back on track. If you 're with me, chip in to make sure we hit our mid-month fundraising goal: | "" | "" | "" | "" | "" | "" "" Thank you, "" Joe | "" "" "" ""

name: Andrew Yang
subject: Growing in New Hampshire
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Andrew Yang
body:
| | Friend --
[](http://my.yang2020.com/?e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=05132019_newsletter_2&n=1) Hello and hope you had a wonderful Mother’s Day weekend! I spent it with both Evelyn and my mom. We should all call our moms more often. On Thursday, Friday, and Saturday, I was back in New Hampshire for our best trip yet. At each stop, I talked about going to high school in New Hampshire and my path from my high school days to running for President. We got our biggest crowds yet: And people noticed. The events were written up in [Manchester](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=KAH_APzQa5gf3fEiVdaEUnfDxHQKdef2gALPHzi_qpAKOVZhaxoBIDw3MUYOoZ9d3aAvR5mub-w6vYm_XXLUXWrLN9lmwXHwmxMLOzzQsMs&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=05132019_newsletter_2&n=2 "Yang starts to attract attention in NH"), [Seacoast Online](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=oVLcRl784-3r1M0lF1t6jzUwrpZsPUNr09oLfgPOTraVFSBUic5LiyVvUUPkd3yM28qNl7YlyD_DRO3GiikjSOoyCmD82x5KCstFrOg7CSdxd4UEbsT8p5Oykbxrquz9&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=05132019_newsletter_2&n=3 "Andrew Yang pitches ‘trickle up’ economy"), [Union Leader](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=GLYJ14ComQvuSO6S8xKqq0bE2QlFhYrnDS2TlDNb_EUIUYlNkytTG07Lp30fTfvICOvX_vftWTm-Y9KPhMih9XkecKUMPuJzYrPQkfTBz5jMxCSf_hrvPNNdaniptIi_Ed0JCWWKwNPinwUp7pm73YDcv-7c0GHCjbiXDIuxPbOydkn6emutlTVzWy-ktzG1RNbZV4LkzATnfmAtkTbS6w&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=05132019_newsletter_2&n=4 "Yang touts his 'tech check' at town hall event
in Hollis"), and other New Hampshire publications, as well as [TIME](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=3nQtyGU6AIRwl-oCWnvkk7B6KvATQEPknywwqp9h0AyQHlUDK5JzxQxmTdNYVcKk00Go9PSpjgTrJnnxkGiDOg&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=05132019_newsletter_2&n=5 "How Andrew Yang's Online Following Has Turned Into a Real-Life
Coalition"), [Fox](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=KVYzWTFUuJ0SsyVZLAg-7KhgymeBWCJ5z6VS6DGe2fJ2DSHO70yLUr9LkY4g1_TQjmfcE7zQ9ZXHcZl2fQi5Cl4l-hTa4ZCXOhyPJhu9F9IukwJJzmVZ4pVv1KropKjorWzTV0u0YqQbQsBqJQzgUA&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=05132019_newsletter_2&n=6 "Once seen as extreme 2020 long shot, Yang is attracting some
buzz"), and [_The New York Times_](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=PiInCVHjZg_Lhb0RQ-dhd0hD1O9x3V4ztV9-WD-oNWxZEoSYJ284GCFx62cQii5_p-KKLz-N3tEVvUk20i3-iez1jPmC4YCVsGWoknhx64w&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=05132019_newsletter_2&n=7 "Is Andrew Yang ‘Too Nice’ to Beat President Trump?"). Among the descriptions: _“Progressive, inclusive, and incredibly creative and smart. His enthusiasm is not divisive ... Yang is one to watch.”_ _“Lightning in a bottle.”_ _"He's my front-runner."_ _"Yang has his eyes on the future."_ _"He's the first political candidate to actually get [many people] excited."_ _"[His campaign is] actually listening to people, taking people seriously, thoughtfully and in an analytical way."_ _" He’s outside the box in a way that, whew, really charged, really evoked amazing insight."_ We also got a mention on Bill Maher as someone on the rise. I’m scheduled to go on Bill Maher in early June. Everywhere we went in New Hampshire, we were met with growing crowds and excitement. I credit Steve Marchand for much of the buzz. Steve ran for governor as a Democrat in 2018 and has now endorsed me. I haven’t been that big a fan of seeking political endorsements—I’m not sure how much they matter to voters, particularly the folks who have embraced our campaign. But in Steve’s case, it is more personal than that; so many people like and admire him as someone they have worked with. At every stop, people greeted him with hugs and smiles. Steve and our NH State Director, Khrystina Snell, have deep relationships all over the state. They will get us in front of the right people on every visit. I have never felt better about our trajectory in New Hampshire. This trip was electric. We will contend and shock the world. This week, we are gearing up for our [biggest rally yet in New York City](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=uuUyPGiVHWR_V3cUbcvCWdikqgOEmv95as43Y58cghf92CPOI7pOoedoIqdOGAc6xUoSty6OWRxsrXn5Uz0RNzDr1f5Qjsj1hZQfbdRmW3g&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=05132019_newsletter_2&n=8 "Andrew Yang's Humanity First Rally in NYC") on Tuesday—ABC and many other major news outlets will be there, and 5,000 supporters have RSVPed. It is going to be epic. And then it’s on to [our first visit to South Carolina](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=c5dAo5qulKAncvNYjq2uGcnHAN92nM1CHInwgA-PIi7HdTRyEez5JIs0AF2zI3zA&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=05132019_newsletter_2&n=9 "Events
Page with South Carolina Events Listed") where we are presently [polling at 2%](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=MzOg9aO7OkKTTvO-SBk1p_OmqSVCITjEjZ3ZzKA1bj7pHCrOrMFKADbeEIw7vSQqlp7rqA_6NlTQbzIeLnCt-H77VEa3qohZSYQciMVfspSkl7ommwJzbYL2Z3MHs8vrdUTiYT7OfqLyuoH7lqvN9XZp2np9YkDVL1UsbiMfyih9GIsm0dBzuZGbXU-z_aokcKz5NgSK5juY7evC8iIRwQ&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=05132019_newsletter_2&n=10 "‘The safe choice:’ Biden widens SC lead in 2020 Democratic
presidential primary"). Your belief in me and the campaign have made all of this possible. Thank you. If you can make a donation today, please do so—we have the chance to make history. Let’s make the most of it. Your charged up candidate, \- Andrew

Most of our donations come from people like you chipping in small amounts when they can. Your continued generosity helps us invest in people.

subject: I just announced:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Julian for the Future
body:
I'm asking you to sign your name before midnight if you agree with my groundbreaking proposal for universal pre-k, tuition-free college, and student loan relief. >> | | |
---
| |
---
| | **BREAKING: Juli an Castro becomes first Presidential candidate to unveil sweeping education plan** | I wanted to tell you personally: I just became the very first Presidential candidate to unveil a comprehensive education plan: **People First Education.** _[**I 'm asking you to sign your name before midnight if you agree with my groundbreaking proposal for universal pre-K, tuition-free college, and student loan relief. >>**](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fcd0b/3eb3e2fc/4e31e66f/7449ba5d/3733180353/VEsH/ "https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/s/education-survey?source=20190513_em_pet_b1_castro_all")_
---

This is personal for me.

Growing up on the West Side of San Antonio, I was never supposed to run for President.

**But I was fortunate to receive quality education that completely changed the course of my life.**

Yet in recent years, I've watched this country's failure to invest in education disproportionately harm lower-income communities like mine.

[_**So today, I just became the first Presidential candidate to unveil a sweeping new education plan -- People First Education. I'm asking you to sign your name if you agree with my revolutionary proposal to enact universal pre-K, tuition-free college, and student loan relief. >>**_](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fcd0b/3eb3e2fc/4e31e66f/7449ba5d/3733180353/VEsE/ "https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/s/education-survey?source=20190513_em_pet_b1_castro_all")

I've experienced personally how education is at the core of the American Dream.

It's a promise we make to every student that if they work hard, they have a shot to succeed -- but our government has failed to live up to its end of the bargain.

**My bold People First Education plan ensures this country restores that promise for every child -- just like it did for me.**

[_**Before midnight, I 'm asking 50,000 Americans to sign their name and take a bold stand for education reform. Can I count on your signature to show your support for People First Education? >>**_](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fcd0b/3eb3e2fc/4e31e66f/7449ba5d/3733180353/VEsF/ "https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/s/education-survey?source=20190513_em_pet_b1_castro_all")

Thank you,

Julian

subject: Why this race matters: Look to Ohio, Georgia, and Alabama...

body:
This is the most important Senate race in the country.
|
---

| | In recent days, we've emailed you about what's at stake in winning this Senate race. Also in recent days, we've seen new assaults on women's reproductive rights in Ohio, Georgia, and Alabama. In Georgia, a law passed barring termination of pregnancy after six weeks (even before some women know they are pregnant), and in Alabama the new law -- not yet signed -- would ban all abortions, remove exceptions for rape and incest, and attach a 99 year sentence to doctors who violate that law.

**We 're talking about life in prison for a doctor who provides a health care procedure to a woman at her request. In America. In 2019.**

Why is this happening?**It 's happening because Donald Trump, Mitch McConnell, and Cory Gardner have worked hand-in-hand to push through far-right judges, **including on the Supreme Court. So now, state lawmakers that do not respect women's rights are scrambling to try to push through extreme legislation. They want these laws to be taken to court, where they count on far-right judges to support them, and **they hope to dismantle the protections of _Roe v. Wade._**

This is what I mean when I talk about the stakes of this election. This is the most important Senate race in the country. We need to elect a new President in 2020. We also need to win the Senate.

When Cory Gardner ran for this seat his supporters used a sexist smear to mock his opponent's staunch defense of reproductive choice. And since then, Gardner's voted time and time again for judges that want to turn back the clock half a century on women's rights, and Gardner has voted repeatedly to torpedo the Affordable Care Act and strip health care coverage from women and men.

My view is simple: **I want every American to have health care. And no American should have the government interfering in health care decisions that she makes with her doctor.**

[**To win this race I need dedicated grassroots supporters like you on my team, because I 'm not taking a dime of corporate PAC money. So I'm reaching out personally today and asking: Can you pitch in just $19? Small donations like yours are what will power our campaign to booting McConnell out of the Majority Leader's office. You can truly make a difference. >>**](https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/m/5fb826ea/232dfd1/91fb900/62a16a82/3160581426/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/baer-email?refcode=20190513_em_fr_b1_baer_all&amount=25&recurring=on&recur_weekly=on") Thank you, Dan

name: Andrew Romanoff
subject: Who we are
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ROMANOFF FOR COLORADO
body:
My family spent Mother's Day together yesterday. Too many families couldn't. |
---
| | | My family spent Mother's Day together yesterday. Too many families couldn't. Since announcing its "zero tolerance" policy one year ago, the Trump Administration has separated thousands of children from their parents. Many have not yet been reunited--a process the administration says could take years. This is not who we are, or at least not who we aspire to be. **[Help me protect the rights of immigrants and refugees.](https://secure.andrewromanoff.com/page/m/7a9848f8/33d1dea4/3b59eeb9/3a3a27a0/3613154689/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/romanoff2020?refcode=EM051319&utm_medium=email&utm_source=romanoff&utm_content=1+-+Help+me+protect+the+rights+of+immigrants&utm_campaign=EM051319&source=EM051319")** Separation damages children in profound and lasting ways. Studies point to an increase in anxiety, depression, PTSD, cancer, asthma, diabetes, and premature death. The President's policies and his rhetoric damage our democracy as well. When the Commander-in-Chief describes asylum seekers as an invading army, muses about machine guns, and jokes about lethal force, he emboldens extremists and vindicates violence. We cannot allow this. **[Will you lend your voice in opposition right now?](https://secure.andrewromanoff.com/page/m/7a9848f8/33d1dea4/3b59eeb9/3a3a27a3/3613154689/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/romanoff2020?refcode=EM051319&utm_medium=email&utm_source=romanoff&utm_content=2+-+Will+you+lend+your+voice+in+opposition+r&utm_campaign=EM051319&source=EM051319")** For me, this cause is personal: I wouldn't be in America if this nation had not opened its doors to my mother and all four of my grandparents. Immigration enabled my family, like so many others, to pursue a better life--and, in turn, to enrich the country we call home. I'll do everything I can to preserve that opportunity for generations to come. Andrew Romanoff

Since announcing its "zero tolerance" policy one year ago, the Trump Administration has separated thousands of children from their parents. Many have not yet been reunited--a process the administration says could take years. This is not who we are, or at least not who we aspire to be. "" Separation damages children in profound and lasting ways. Studies point to an increase in anxiety, depression, PTSD, cancer, asthma, diabetes, and premature death. The President's policies and his rhetoric damage our democracy as well. When the Commander-in-Chief describes asylum seekers as an invading army, muses about machine guns, and jokes about lethal force, he emboldens extremists and vindicates violence. We cannot allow this. "" For me, this cause is personal: I wouldn't be in America if this nation had not opened its doors to my mother and all four of my grandparents. Immigration enabled my family, like so many others, to pursue a better life--and, in turn, to enrich the country we call home. name: Amy O'Rourke
subject: Grateful
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY BETO FOR AMERICA
body:
[1]Beto for America

This past weekend was my first chance to join Beto on the road since we
kicked off the campaign almost two months ago. Beto and I talk every night
when he’s on the road, but I wanted to feel the energy for myself and meet
the people he’s been telling me about.

I flew to Boston on Thursday to meet Beto, Cynthia, and Chris who had just
flown in from a week of campaigning in Texas, Iowa, and Minnesota. I took
the wheel to drive from Boston to Salem, New Hampshire, the first of many
energizing stops.

From Salem to Hookset, New London in Sawyer Colby College’s incredible
library, Hanover at Dartmouth, Lebanon, Manchester, Bedford, Exeter, and
Dover, this campaign trip felt very much like what I loved most during our
Senate campaign in Texas. We were meeting people who care deeply about
this country, who take their civic responsibility seriously, and who want
to share their wealth of knowledge with those who seek to hold public
office.

Much like Texas, I have been impressed with the number of young people who
have come out — asking the tough questions, organizing on their campuses,
signing up to volunteer for our campaign, or running in their own right.
In Hanover, I met Garrett Muscatel, who is both a junior at Dartmouth
College and a recently elected state representative. In the last few
years, the New Hampshire Young Dems have activated 7,000 young people
across the state and helped get Garrett and dozens of young Democrats
elected to the State House. The power of young people is palpable, and
they will be the force behind progressive, bold changes in our country.

At the conclusion of each of our town halls in New Hampshire, we stayed to
meet everyone who came out. Some wanted to take pictures, others wanted to
ask a follow up question or share a personal story. Understanding the
seriousness with which voters in New Hampshire take their responsibility
to screen the Democratic presidential candidates (I met one high school
student that had already met 13 candidates!), we have been pleasantly
surprised by the number of people who told us that we have their support.
People like Oye and his son, Ulysses, both passionate about fairness and
equity, have stepped up to share their expertise and time for this
campaign. Kaitlyn, a school principal, is already spending her evenings
and weekends as Super Volunteer at our events. Ruth retired early because
she feels just as strongly that this is the most important election of her
lifetime, and she now wants to get behind Beto.

This weekend has been a reminder to not read too much into the latest news
story, the latest poll, but to keep focused on why we are running and how
we want to run. Like we did in Texas, we are putting a premium on meeting
people, engaging in authentic, honest dialogue, and relying on those we
meet to step up and lead this campaign.

I flew home in time to spend Mother’s Day dinner with my babies, my dad,
and my mom (who so graciously took care of them this weekend). But I can’t
wait to get out on the campaign trail again soon. I am grateful for the
people who have stepped up to volunteer and contribute, those who are
working and sacrificing to ensure we meet our ambitious goals for this
country.

Many thanks,

Amy

\--------------------
From: Our Revolution
Subject: Supporting these women now could make them the next political superstars
Date: Sunday, May 12, 2019 10:09 am

Friends,
This Mother's Day weekend, Our Revolution wants you to meet five progressive women who are running for office and putting families first. Once you've been introduced, we're going to ask you to make a donation to fund Our Revolution's work electing them and more progressive candidates across the country.
[](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7a525/bed43eb/960787b5/75fb7ae3/1068148415/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ourrev-women?refcode=em190513snd&noskip=true")
_Tiffany Cab an, Helen Gym, Erika Almiron, Jackie Goldberg, and Yasmine Taeb_
**Tiffany Cab an** is the only public defender running for **Queens County District Attorney** on a basic premise: imprisonment should not be the first solution to social problems. Our society spends $143,000 per prisoner to lock them up. Tiffany wants to put that money toward jobs and education, not jails and incarceration.
Her race is a continuation of our fight to make the Empire State more just. Just last fall, we swept the so-called Independent Democratic Conference out of power in the New York Senate with 6 Our Revolution-endorsed state senators. Let's take the next step revolutionizing New York City and the criminal justice systems of these major American cities.
**Yasmine Taeb** is running for the **Virginia Senate** on a platform of protecting a woman's right to an abortion, the passage of the Equal Rights Amendment, and paid parental leave - an issue that her opponent ignores. Control of the Virginia Senate may swing to Democrats in this fall's election, and we need to make sure progressives are in power to drive the agenda in a unified state government - electing Yasmine is a critical piece of that mission.
**Helen Gym** and **Erika Almir on** are running for **Philadelphia City Council.**
Helen is an activist who co-founded Parents United for Public Education, and in her first term on the city council, she led the effort to pass a Fair Work Week law so that 130,000 workers could have stable schedules, a path to full-time work, and the opportunity to support themselves and their loved ones.
Erika is the daughter of house cleaners and a champion of the Domestic Workers' Bill of Rights, which includes overtime, paid sick leave, and fair contract protection. Together, Helen and Erika will give Philadelphia progressives a voice on the City Council _and_ the District Attorney's office with Larry Krasner, who has transformed Philadelphia's criminal justice system in part thanks to your help electing him as an Our Revolution candidate in 2017.
**Jackie Goldberg** is running for the **Los Angeles School Board** on a platform of promoting public schools and fighting for social services within educational institutions. In America's second largest city, electing champions for public schools will have an impact on millions of families.
Together, these candidates running for local and state government have the opportunity to affect change for millions of people in their communities. Putting your support behind local races is critical. We're not just going to elect a president, we're going to lead a political revolution.
**[Are you ready to help elect these women? Make a donation to Our Revolution today.](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7a525/bed43eb/960787b5/75fb7ae3/1068148415/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ourrev-women?refcode=em190513snd&noskip=true")**

Thanks for reading, and for your support of these extraordinary women.
In Solidarity,
The Team at Our Revolution

body:
Each May, our country honors Asian-American and Pacific Islander Heritage Month, a time to reflect on the remarkable accomplishments... | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=9a65294272&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • May 13, 2019
---
| | |
# **An incredible reason to celebrate this May**

**Each May,** our country honors Asian-American and Pacific Islander Heritage Month, a time to reflect on the remarkable accomplishments of these communities and their special role in helping to define the history and successes of the United States.

**This year** , there is extra cause for celebration. The soaring American economy of 2019 is benefitting a number of historically marginalized groups, such as African-Americans and Hispanics. The Asian-American community is no exception: Unemployment for this segment has reached the lowest levels _ever_ recorded—an astonishing 2.1 percent last month.

**President Donald J. Trump** wants to build on that economic empowerment. Today, he [signed an Executive Order](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=f72f792d29&e=3ee4985eea) to establish the Advisory Commission on Asian Americans and Pacific Islanders (AAPI), along with a White House initiative to help broaden access to more employers and other economic resources for this community.

**“The contributions** of Americans of Asian and Pacific Islander descent are firmly woven into the diverse fabric of our Nation,” President Trump said in a recent proclamation. “These Americans bolster our economy as entrepreneurs, business owners, and employees who initiate and expand opportunities for their families, communities, and country.”

[Surya Gunasekara _:_ Asian Americans keep winning in the Trump economy](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=9691d0711d&e=3ee4985eea)

**Read:** [President Trump’s Proclamation on Asian American and Pacific Islander Heritage Month](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=635c78acca&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|
## **President Trump hosts the Prime Minister of Hungary**

**As a member of NATO,** Hungary is an important ally for the United States on both national security and economic matters. Today, President Trump hosted Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban in the Oval Office, deepening cooperation on a range of crucial issues such as trade, energy, and cybersecurity.

**The two leaders** also honored the 20th anniversary of Hungary officially joining NATO.

**“We are proud** that so many Hungarians contributed to the tremendous progress of United States,” Prime Minister Orban said, adding that the two countries face many similar challenges at home and abroad. “We are proud to stand together with the United States on fighting against illegal migration, on terrorism, and to protect and help the Christian communities all around the world.”

[Watch: President Trump and Prime Minister Orban in the Oval Office](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=fc39b5f915&e=3ee4985eea)
